LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SAM SHULMAN AND AGGE, INC. DBA BLACK AND WHITE CHECKER CAB'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS NOS. 35, 39 AND 47 ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT NOTES FACT NO. 34 IS NOT DISPUTED. THE UNDERLYING CITED DISCOVERY ITEMS CAN BE FAIRLY READ TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT'S TAXI CAB WAS OPERATING IN EXCESS OF THE CONCEDED SPEED LIMIT JUST BEFORE THE ACCIDENT, AND MAKING INFERENCES IN FAVOR OF THE PARTY OPPOSING THIS MOTION (AS I AM REQUIRED TO DO), I INFER A SIMILAR SPEED AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DO NOT PRESENT INDEPENDENT GROUNDS, AND ACCORDINGLY IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SAM SHULMAN AND AGGE, INC. DBA BLACK AND WHITE CHECKER CAB's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. The objections to Plaintiff's separate statement of facts nos. 35, 39 and 47 are overruled. The Court notes Fact No. 34 is not disputed. The underlying cited discovery items can be fairly read to show that defendant's taxi cab was operating in excess of the conceded speed limit just before the accident, and making inferences in favor of the party opposing this motion (as I am required to do), I infer a similar speed at the time of the accident. The motion for summary adjudication do not present independent grounds, and accordingly is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
